source-discipline.reason: v10.14 reformulation grounded in concrete artifacts: scripts/bootstrap.sh exists in this commit and was smoke-tested end-to-end against /mnt/project/GitHub_pat (HTTP 200 fetches across 5 critical files, DB query returns 670/670 dynamic ratios, header grep finds 3 expected section names, exit code 0). The before/after table is verifiable by comparing v10.13 inline-bootstrap to v10.14 thin-caller diff. The architecture v2.3 extraction guidance is cited verbatim.

bias_direction: v10.14 was rewritten mid-session in response to owner frustration with PI bump frequency. The bias risk is toward over-claiming what extraction solves: not all PI bumps are bootstrap-related, so the 'last bootstrap-related PI bump' framing is true narrowly but does not mean PI bumps disappear. Rule changes, skill assignments, and override clauses will still require bumps. The before/after workflow table addresses this honestly by listing what STAYS in the PI bump column.
independent_reviewer_counterclaim: An outside reviewer could object that extracting bootstrap to a script trades one form of fragility (PI bump-and-paste) for another (network dependency at session start to fetch the script from main). If main is unreachable, the entire bootstrap fails. Counter: the existing inline bootstrap already needs network to fetch sessions/LATEST, project-standards.md, the session record, skill-registry, workplan, and the DB — all 6 critical fetches require network. Fetching the bootstrap script itself is one additional fetch with the same failure mode, not a new class of risk.
